title: IR-PB-04: Ransomware Containment
content: Upon confirmation of lateral movement indicators or mass encryption events, isolate affected endpoints at the network switch level before initiating credential rotation to prevent attacker persistence. Do not reboot hosts to preserve memory forensics.

title: IR-PB-07: Account Compromise
content: If a user account is suspected of compromise via phishing, immediately reset the Active Directory credentials and revoke any active session tokens in the identity provider. Analyze email forwarding rules for persistence.

title: IR-PB-12: Insider Threat Data Exfiltration
content: For suspected insider data exfiltration, prioritize endpoint isolation to halt ongoing transfers. Preserve the local device for forensic imaging, particularly tracking USB connection history and browser cache records. Notify legal and HR teams immediately.

title: T1048: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
content: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol than that of the existing command and control channel. The data may also be sent to an alternate network location from the main C2 server.

title: T1486: Data Encrypted for Impact
content: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems or on large numbers of systems in a network to interrupt availability to system and network resources. They can attempt to render stored data inaccessible by encrypting files or volumes.

title: Policy-12: Critical Severity Escalation
content: Any security incident classified as Critical Severity (involving data exfiltration, ransomware, or domain compromise) must trigger an immediate paging alert to the SOC Lead and the on-call Incident Commander. Destructive containment actions require SOC Lead approval unless pre-authorized by an automated playbook.

title: Policy-05: Removable Media Use
content: Connecting unauthorized or personal USB storage devices to enterprise endpoints is strictly prohibited. EDR agents are configured to log all USB mount events and block writes to unencrypted mass storage devices.
